title: Trust Deed Review Field Dictionary
purpose: Canonical field, evidence and drafting guardrail reference for the no-action trust deed review GPT.

linked_clause_fields.description: Vesting, termination or perpetuity-period provision reference.
Latest vesting date, termination date or formula/mechanism by which trust assets must vest.
Trust income, net income or distributable income definition.
Trustee power or obligation to distribute, pay or accumulate income.
Power to stream or classify income.
Power to determine, allocate or advance capital.
Unit register or unitholder definition for unit trusts.
Clause supporting the method of distribution.
Clause requiring resolution before a specific date, including before 30 June.
Power to amend or vary the deed.

checklist_output.helper_text_policy: Do not show helper text, JSON paths or internal mapping notes to the user.

trust_type_extraction.discretionary: Discretionary/family trust wording and beneficiary-class concepts without substantive unit-capital language.
trust_type_extraction.do_not_classify_as_unit_if: The only unit wording is in investment powers referring to acquiring units in another trust or company.
trust_type_extraction.unclear: If both concepts appear or evidence is unclear, mark trust type as Review and ask the user to confirm.

vesting_extraction.goal: Identify the latest date or mechanism by which trust assets vest.
vesting_extraction.calculation_rule: Calculate or state a calendar date only if the deed date, governing law and formula clearly support it.
vesting_extraction.uncertain_formula_rule: If a formula depends on uncertain governing law, lives in being, commencement legislation or another unresolved fact, record the formula and mark it for review.

perpetuity_guide.New South Wales: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Victoria: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Queensland: 125 years from the day the disposition is made unless the trust terms state or imply a shorter period. The 125-year regime commenced on 1 August 2025.
perpetuity_guide.South Australia: No fixed statutory perpetuity period; rules against perpetuities and excessive accumulations are abolished.
perpetuity_guide.Western Australia: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Tasmania: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Australian Capital Territory: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Northern Territory: Lives in being plus 21 years, or 80 years from settlement taking effect, whichever the settlement specifies; if none is specified, 80 years applies.

review_blockers: Corporate trustee details are missing where a corporate trustee is required.
Vesting date or vesting clause is missing.
Income definition or distribution power clause is missing.
A deed amendment is noted but the amended deed is not included.
drafting_guardrails: Use Clause not found where the deed does not disclose a clause.
Preserve evidence and confidence for practitioner review.
Generated checklists and summaries are drafting aids for review by an Australian legal practitioner.
